turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Alito has the opinion of the Court in case 23-1270, Riley versus Bondi.

turns.name: JUSTICE ALITO
turns.text: This case concerns the deadline for contesting removal from this country. Under the immigration laws, such a petition must be filed within 30 days of a "final order of removal." In this case, the Fourth Circuit held that this 30-day rule was jurisdictional and it therefore dismissed petitioner Riley's petition for review. We now decide two questions. First, the 30-day deadline cannot be satisfied by filing a petition for review within 30 days of a Board of Immigration Appeals order in a withholding only proceeding. That is a proceeding in which the issue is not whether an alien will be removed, but where he or she will be sent. As we have previously said, such an order is not a final order of removal. Second, we hold that the 30-day filing rule is a mandatory claims processing rule, not a jurisdictional rule. The government has told us that it does not plan to pursue the timeliness issue, so we vacate the judgment below and remand for further proceedings. The opinion of the Court is joined in full by the Chief Justice and Justices Thomas, Kavanaugh, and Barrett. Justices Sotomayor, Kagan, Gorsuch and Jackson join in part. Justice Thomas has filed a concurring opinion. Justice Sotomayor has filed an opinion dissenting in part that is joined in full by Justices Kagan and Jackson, and joined except as to Part IV by Justice Gorsuch.
